ddls:
CREATE TABLE "production_lines" (
  "production_line_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production line */,
  "production_line_name" TEXT /* Name of the production line */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the production line */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the production line */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the production line (e.g., active, maintenance, offline) */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "production_line_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum production capacity of the line */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("production_line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "kpis" (
  "kpi_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each KPI */,
  "production_line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line the KPI belongs to */,
  "kpi_name" TEXT /* Name of the KPI (e.g., Result-A, Result-B) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the KPI */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the KPI */,
  "normal_range_min" REAL /* Minimum value of the normal range for the KPI */,
  "normal_range_max" REAL /* Maximum value of the normal range for the KPI */,
  "alert_threshold_min" REAL /* Minimum threshold for triggering an alert */,
  "alert_threshold_max" REAL /* Maximum threshold for triggering an alert */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the KPI data (e.g., sensor, machine, manual input) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("kpi_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_kpis_production_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("production_line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("production_line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "production_line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line the measurement belongs to */,
  "kpi_id" INTEGER /* ID of the KPI being measured */,
  "measurement_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the measurement */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the KPI measurement */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that took the measurement */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine that the sensor is attached to */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who recorded the measurement (if applicable) */,
  "measurement_status" TEXT /* Status of the measurement (e.g., valid, invalid, suspect) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_production_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("production_line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("production_line_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_kpi_id FOREIGN KEY ("kpi_id") REFERENCES kpis ("kpi_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., operator, engineer, manager) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "default_permission" TEXT /* Default permission level for users with this role (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "department_access" TEXT /* Departments the role has access to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role the permission belongs to */,
  "production_line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line the permission applies to */,
  "kpi_id" INTEGER /* ID of the KPI the permission applies to */,
  "permission_type" TEXT /* Type of permission (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read, write, delete, admin) */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if this permission is the default for the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_kpi_id FOREIGN KEY ("kpi_id") REFERENCES kpis ("kpi_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_production_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("production_line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("production_line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "production_line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line the alert belongs to */,
  "kpi_id" INTEGER /* ID of the KPI the alert applies to */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the alert */,
  "message" TEXT /* Alert message */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved, dismissed) */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert was resolved */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_production_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("production_line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("production_line_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_kpi_id FOREIGN KEY ("kpi_id") REFERENCES kpis ("kpi_id")
)
